spec_match.rationale: The lab detects the affected `xml-stylesheet` to SVG pattern and exercises the replacement APIs named on-page: `XSLTProcessor`, `DOMParser.parseFromString(markup, "image/svg+xml")`, and `document.createElementNS`.

interactive.rationale: The concept now has editable XML/XSLT textareas, five sample buttons, an affected-pattern analyzer, a live XSLTProcessor runner, a DOMParser SVG replacement runner, and live rendered output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still covers the three migration paths from the feature index and now demonstrates their runtime behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The lab includes non-SVG XSLT output, XML without a stylesheet processing instruction, SVG output missing the SVG namespace, and malformed XML parser-error handling.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148 on the local server. The migration guide loaded with editable XML/XSLT controls and five presets. Exercised SVG output, HTML output, no stylesheet PI, missing SVG namespace, malformed XML, Analyze affected pattern, Run XSLTProcessor, and Run DOMParser replacement. Observed affected/not-affected state changes, SVG rendering for valid SVG output, non-applicable handling for HTML output, wrong-namespace failure text, malformed XML parser-error text, and conformance 7/7. Network requests returned 200/204; the only console output was Chrome's expected XSLT deprecation warning.
